title: SumSub Forgery client reset
description: How to check Forgery in SumSub and reset the client's verification if needed.
when_to_use: Use this process when a client has a Forgery flag in SumSub and you need to check the reason and decide whether the client's verification should be reset.
warning: Reset the applicant only when you cannot independently confirm the document authenticity and the internal process allows reset.

steps.title: Open Applicants - Individuals
steps.text: In SumSub, open the left-side menu and go to Applicants -> Individuals.

steps.title: Search for the client
steps.text: Enter the client ID in the search bar at the top of the Individuals page. After the result appears, hover over the client row/link and open the client page.

steps.title: Check Notes
steps.text: On the client page, check the Notes section. It contains a short description of the Forgery type.

steps.title: Generate AI overview if needed
steps.text: Find the Overview by Summy AI Copilot section and click Generate overview if you need an additional AI summary.

steps.title: Check documents marked as Forgery
steps.text: Go to Documents. Check Identity document and Other documents / Proof of address. Scroll until you find the document marked with the Forgery label.

steps.title: Decide whether reset is needed
steps.text: Forgery can be marked on ID or POA. Review the document and decide whether it is possible to independently confirm its authenticity. If not possible, proceed with client reset.

steps.title: Open reset menu
steps.text: At the top of the page, find the three-dot menu to the right of Change applicant status and click it.

steps.title: Select Reset applicant
steps.text: In the dropdown menu, select Reset applicant.

steps.title: Confirm reset
steps.text: In the confirmation window, click Reset applicant again.

steps.title: Verification is reset
steps.text: The client's verification is reset. In the Client Area, the client will be able to pass verification again if needed.
